# Vue & Node admin panel framework

> Launch robust back-office apps faster with AdminForth’s easy setup and customization

This file contains links to documentation sections following the llmstxt.org standard.

## Table of Contents

- [Getting Started](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/001-gettingStarted.md): This page provides a step-by-step guide to quickly get started with AdminForth using the `adminforth` CLI.
- [Hello world app without CLI](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/hello-world.md): While AdminForth CLI is the fastest way to create a new project, you can also create a new project manually.
- [Glossary](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/02-glossary.md): A DataSource is a connection to one database. Datasources have id for references from resources and URL which follows the standard URI format. For ...
- [Branding and Theming](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/01-branding.md): Learn how to customize the branding and theming of your AdminForth application.
- [Custom record field rendering](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/02-customFieldRendering.md): Let's change how AdminForth renders the number of rooms in the 'list' and 'show' views.
- [Virtual columns](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/03-virtualColumns.md): Sometimes you need to visualize custom columns which do not exist in database. 
- [Hooks](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/04-hooks.md): Hooks are powerful tools to modify the data before it is saved to the database, execute something after data were saved or deleted, change the quer...
- [Limiting actions access](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/05-limitingAccess.md): As you might have noticed in diagrams from [adminforth hooks](./04-hooks.md) section of this tutorial, AdminForth checks `options.allowedActions` b...
- [Custom pages](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/06-customPages.md): Learn how to create custom pages in AdminForth.
- [Alerts and confirmations](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/07-alert.md): When you are writing custom components or pages you might need to show alerts or confirmations to the user.
- [Page Injections](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/08-pageInjections.md): In addition to ability to create custom pages and overwrite how fields are rendered, you can also inject custom components in standard AdminForth p...
- [Actions](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/09-Actions.md): You might need to give admin users a feature to perform some action on a single record. Actions can be displayed as buttons in the list view and/or...
- [Menu & Header](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/10-menuConfiguration.md): Adminforth uses [Iconify](https://iconify.design/) icons everywhere, including the menu.
- [Data API](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/11-dataApi.md): AdminForth Data API is a minimal set of methods to manipulate the data in the database. 
- [Security](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/12-security.md): Security and privacy if adminforth users is one of the most important aspects of AdminForth.
- [Standard pages tuning](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/13-standardPagesTuning.md): In some cases, you may want to organize data fields into specific groups for better structure and clarity. For example, you could create a "Main In...
- [AdminForth Components Library](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/15-afcl.md): AFCL is a set of components which you can use as build blocks in your AdminForth application. AFCL allows to keep the design consistent with minima...
- [Websocket](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/03-Customization/16-websocket.md): AdminForth provide own build-in websocket interface which allows to stream some data to frontend from backend.
- [Deploy in Docker](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/04-deploy.md): In general you can already run your `index.ts` file which we created in [Getting Started](/docs/tutorial/001-gettingStarted.md)
- [Email Adapters](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/05-Adapters/01-email-adapters.md): Used to send emails.
- [OAuth2 Adapters](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/05-Adapters/02-oauth2-adapters.md): Used to authenticate users via OAuth 2.0 providers.
- [Image Generation Adapters](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/05-Adapters/03-image-generation-adapters.md): Used for image-generating AI tools.
- [Storage Adapters](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/05-Adapters/04-storage-adapters.md): Used for storing files.
- [AI Completion Adapters](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/05-Adapters/05-ai-completion-adapters.md): Used for AI-powered text completion.
- [Image Analysis Adapters](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/05-Adapters/06-image-analysis-adapters.md): Used for AI-powered image analysis.
- [Key-value Adapters](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/05-Adapters/07-key-value-adapters.md): Key-value adapters are used to store data in a key-value format. They provide a simple and efficient way to manage data where quick access to value...
- [Captcha Adapters](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/05-Adapters/08-captcha-adapters.md): Used to add captcha to the login screen.
- [CLI Commands](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/06-CLICommands.md): > **CLI Reference**: A list of all available CLI commands for AdminForth.
- [Usage of the logger](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/07-UsageOfLogger.md): There are cases when you might want to debug an Adminforth app or add custom logs.
- [Agent](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/agent.md): > TODO: this plugin tutorial is in progress, some information might be missing, we are actively working on it now. If you have any questions regard...
- [Two-Factor Authentication Plugin](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/two-factors-auth.md): The Two-Factor Authentication Plugin provides an additional layer of security to the application by requiring users to provide a second form of aut...
- [Foreign Inline List](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/foreign-inline-list.md): Foreign inline list plugin allows to display a list (table) of items from a foreign table in the show view.
- [Audit Log](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/audit-log.md): AuditLog plugin allows to log all changes in the resources done from the admin panel. 
- [Upload](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/upload.md): This plugin allows you to upload files to Amazon S3 bucket.
- [Upload API](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/08-Plugins/05-1-upload-api.md): The Upload plugin exposes an API for both backend-only uploads and direct browser uploads using presigned URLs. You can:
- [Markdown](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/markdown.md): The Markdown plugin enables Markdown support in AdminForth, allowing users to create and edit fields using Markdown syntax and save rich content in...
- [Email Password Reset](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/email-password-reset.md): Plugin allows to reset password for admin users who forgot their password by sending email with reset link signed with secured JWT token.
- [Import Export](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/import-export.md): Import-Export is a plugin that allows you to import and export data from and to a CSV file.
- [Open Signup](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/open-signup.md): Open Signup plugin allows users to register in adminforth by them-selfs without admin. 
- [Internationalization (i18n)](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/i18n.md): This plugin allows you translate your AdminForth application to multiple languages.
- [OAuth Authentication](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/oauth.md): The OAuth plugin enables OAuth2-based authentication in AdminForth, allowing users to sign in using their Google, GitHub, Facebook or other OAuth2 ...
- [Inline Create](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/inline-create.md): The Inline Create plugin enables inline create in AdminForth, allowing users to create records directly in the list view.
- [List In-Place Edit](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/list-in-place-edit.md): The List in Place Edit plugin allows you to edit records directly in the list view.
- [Text Complete](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/text-complete.md): This plugin allows you to auto-complete text and string fields using OpenAI GPT models via the Responses API.
- [Foreign Inline Show](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/foreign-inline-show.md): Allows to add an inline show of connected foreign resource records on a adminforth show page.
- [Email Invite](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/email-invite.md): Email Invite plugin allows administrators to create users without setting passwords. Instead, the plugin sends an email invitation to the newly cre...
- [Bulk AI Flow](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/bulk-ai-flow.md): This plugin allows filling fields in multiple selected records based on data from other fields using LLM.
- [Universal Search (Legacy)](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/universal-search.md): Ephemeral, debounced multi‑column search for List pages. A lightweight input (injected at `beforeActionButtons`) sends the term with each list requ...
- [Login Captcha](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/login-captcha.md): (this plugin experemental and not yet fully tested)
- [User Soft Delete](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/user-soft-delete.md): Allows to deactivate users accound without deleting it.
- [Clone Row](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/clone-row.md): Allows to create a copy of record quickly, by substituting default values to create form
- [Many to Many](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/many2many.md): This plugin lets you manage many-to-many relationships.
- [Background Jobs](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/background-jobs.md): First, install the plugin:
- [Quick Filters](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/quick-filters.md): First, install the plugin:
- [Auto Remove Plugin](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/auto-remove.md): This plugin removes records from resources based on **count-based** or **time-based** rules.
- [Rich Editor](https://adminforth.dev/tutorial/Plugins/rich-editor.md): Under the hood this plugin uses [Quill](https://quilljs.com/). Quill is a free, open source WYSIWYG editor built for the modern web.
- [Plugin development guide](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/09-Advanced/01-plugin-development.md): Creating a plugin is a powerful way to extend AdminForth functionality.
- [Working without direct database connection](https://adminforth.dev/docs/tutorial/09-Advanced/02-working-without-direct-database-connection.md): Out of the box, AdminForth connects directly to your database using one of the supported drivers (PostgreSQL, MySQL, ClickHouse, MongoDB) and execu...
